skill.name: kotlin-coroutines-expert
skill.description: Expert patterns for Kotlin Coroutines and Flow, covering structured concurrency, error handling, and testing.
skill.summary: Expert patterns for Kotlin Coroutines and Flow, covering structured concurrency, error handling, and...
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as shell execution and Kotlin Flow references as weak cryptography. Manual review found only prose and Kotlin examples, with no executable scripts, network access, filesystem access, environment access, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Misread as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the locations are inline Markdown code spans and fenced Kotlin examples. No shell command execution instruction or script invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens appear only in Markdown formatting and Kotlin snippets. The file contains no runnable shell, Ruby, or command execution directives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Kotlin Flow Misread as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptography, but the flagged text refers to Kotlin Flow, a reactive stream API. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security-sensitive crypto use appears in the reviewed lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context is a coroutine and Flow description, not cryptographic code. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.

content.value_statement: Kotlin coroutine bugs can cause leaks, hanging tests, and confusing cancellation behavior. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured concurrency, Flow design, error handling, and coroutine testing.
actual_capabilities: Explains structured concurrency patterns using CoroutineScope, coroutineScope, and supervisorScope.
Guides exception handling choices for top-level scopes and suspending functions.
Distinguishes StateFlow, SharedFlow, and cold Flow usage for reactive Kotlin code.
Shows parallel async work with supervisorScope and explicit await behavior.
Recommends coroutine testing practices with TestScope, runTest, and injected TestDispatcher.
limitations: It is a guidance-only Markdown skill and does not execute Kotlin code.
It does not inspect a project automatically without additional user-provided files.
It does not replace official kotlinx.coroutines documentation for API changes.
It provides examples, not a complete application architecture.

use_cases.title: Design an Async Feature
use_cases.description: Plan coroutine scopes, dispatcher usage, and Flow streams before implementing a Kotlin feature.

use_cases.title: Debug Cancellation Problems
use_cases.description: Review coroutine cancellation, exception propagation, and supervisorScope behavior in existing code.

use_cases.title: Improve Coroutine Tests
use_cases.description: Convert fragile coroutine tests into deterministic tests using runTest and controlled dispatchers.

prompt_templates.title: Explain a Coroutine Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how I should use structured concurrency for this Kotlin use case. Focus on scope ownership, cancellation, and simple examples.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Flow Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide between Flow, StateFlow, and SharedFlow for this feature. Explain the tradeoffs and recommended implementation shape.

prompt_templates.title: Review Coroutine Error Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this coroutine code for exception handling and cancellation issues. Identify risky patterns and propose safer alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor and Test Coroutine Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this coroutine workflow for structured concurrency and deterministic tests. Include dispatcher injection, runTest strategy, and failure behavior.

output_examples.input: Review a ViewModel that launches multiple network calls in parallel.
output_examples.output: A review explaining scope ownership, cancellation behavior, supervisorScope tradeoffs, and safer error handling steps.

output_examples.input: Choose the right stream type for search UI state and one-time navigation events.
output_examples.output: A recommendation that separates retained UI state from transient events, with clear guidance on StateFlow and SharedFlow usage.

output_examples.input: Fix a coroutine test that sometimes hangs.
output_examples.output: A test strategy describing runTest, virtual time, TestDispatcher injection, and checks for uncompleted child coroutines.

best_practices: Keep coroutine ownership explicit through lifecycle-aware scopes.
Inject dispatchers so production code and tests can control execution.
Treat cancellation as normal control flow and rethrow CancellationException.
anti_patterns: Using GlobalScope for work that should follow a lifecycle.
Catching broad exceptions without preserving cancellation semantics.
Mixing retained state and one-time events in a single stream.

faq.question: Does this skill run Kotlin code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, review prompts, and implementation patterns for Kotlin coroutine work.

faq.question: Can it help with Android ViewModel code?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for viewModelScope, Flow state, cancellation, and coroutine testing.

faq.question: Does it cover Kotlin Flow?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Flow transformations, StateFlow, SharedFlow, and dispatcher placement.

faq.question: Can it debug hanging tests?
faq.answer: It can guide analysis of common causes, including missing runTest setup and uncontrolled dispatchers.

faq.question: Is this skill tied to one IDE?
faq.answer: No. It is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: Does it replace official coroutine documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use it for applied guidance and review, while checking official docs for API details.
